early_access_text: The main functions of GrowerHub are already available for free and without a card. Connect equipment, assemble zones and customize controls at your own pace. If you need help, the GrowerHub team is nearby on Telegram.

start.title: How to get started with GrowerHub
start.description: A short self-service path from a Zigbee coordinator to your first device, zone and GrowerHub dashboard.
start.intro: Log in via Yandex or Google, connect Zigbee2MQTT and assemble your zones from automatically found devices. Plants and additional settings can be added later.

steps.title: Login
steps.text: Use Yandex or Google. No card needed.

steps.title: Create a connection
steps.text: Give the coordinator any clear name.

steps.title: Select mode
steps.text: The new installation is connected directly; existing Home Assistant retains local MQTT via local bridge.

steps.title: Run package
steps.text: Select Windows, Raspberry Pi/Linux, or download the configuration for manual installation.

steps.title: See devices
steps.text: GrowerHub automatically shows devices and the metrics exposed by Zigbee2MQTT.

steps.title: Create a zone
steps.text: Specify only the name and distribute the devices. Plants are added as desired.

steps.title: Open dashboard
steps.text: View data and manage permitted functions. Turn on automation later.

minimum.title: Minimum to start
minimum.monitoring: Always-on computer or Raspberry Pi, supported Zigbee coordinator and one temperature/humidity sensor.
minimum.control: For control, add a Zigbee smart plug. Soil sensors, leak sensors and other equipment can be connected later.
minimum.existing: If Zigbee2MQTT and Home Assistant are already running, the local bridge will leave MQTT in place and transfer only the necessary topics to GrowerHub.
